title: Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Assam

overview.short: Bherjan Bokajan Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in Tinsukia district of Assam located in India covering 7.22 km2.
overview.description: Bherjan Bokajan Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in Tinsukia district of Assam located in India covering 7.22 km2. This wildlife sanctuary is spread across three blocks located in Tinsukia district of Upper Assam which consist of three separate forests, namely Bherjan, Borajan and Padumoni. It is a very important forest in terms of conservation and includes habitat for animals such as hoolock gibbon, capped langur, pig-tailed macaque, slow loris, rhesus macaque, leopard, etc. 84 species of birds were recorded here.

topPlaces.name: Tinsukia
topPlaces.description: Tinsukia (Pron: ˌtɪnˈsʊkiə) is an industrial city. It is situated 480 kilometres (298 mi) north-east of Guwahati and 84 kilometres (52 mi) away from the border with Arunachal Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Lido Town
topPlaces.description: Lido Town is a census town in Tinsukia district in the Indian state of Assam.

topPlaces.name: Lido Tikok
topPlaces.description: Lido Tikok is a census town in Tinsukia district in the Indian state of Assam.

topPlaces.name: Maguri Motapung Beel
topPlaces.description: Maguri Motapung Beel (also known as Maguri Motapung Bill, Maguri Bill and Maguri Beel) is a wetland and lake located near Dibru-Saikhowa National Park and Motapung Village of Tinsukia district in Assam. Maguri Motapung Beel serves as a natural home to wildlife and provide a source of livelihood to the local communities.

topPlaces.name: Railway Heritage Park, Tinsukia
topPlaces.description: The Railway Heritage Park is a railway museum at New Tinsukia Junction railway station, in Assam, India, opened in 2010. The museum is bordered by National Highway 37.

topPlaces.name: Baghjan
topPlaces.description: Baghjan Gaon is a village under the Doom Dooma subdivision of the Tinsukia district in Assam, India. As per the 2011 Census of India, Baghjan Gaon has a total population of 4,488 people including 2,244 males and 2,244 females.

topPlaces.name: Makum
topPlaces.description: Makum (IPA: mɑːkəm) is a town and a town area committee in Tinsukia district in the Indian state of Assam. It should not be mistaken for Makum Pathar (the place near Digboi where, crude oil was first struck in Asia).

topPlaces.name: 2020 Assam gas and oil leak
topPlaces.description: The 2020 Assam gas and oil leak, also referred as the Baghjan gas leak, was a blowout and methane leak that happened in Oil India Limited's Baghjan Oilfield in Tinsukia district, Assam, India on 27 May 2020. The blowout occurred at Well No.

items.note: Tinsukia (Pron: ˌtɪnˈsʊkiə) is an industrial city. It is situated 480 kilometres (298 mi) north-east of Guwahati and 84 kilometres (52 mi) away from the border with Arunachal Pradesh.
Lido Town is a census town in Tinsukia district in the Indian state of Assam.
Lido Tikok is a census town in Tinsukia district in the Indian state of Assam.

Maguri Motapung Beel (also known as Maguri Motapung Bill, Maguri Bill and Maguri Beel) is a wetland and lake located near Dibru-Saikhowa National Park and Motapung Village of Tinsukia district in Assam. Maguri Motapung Beel serves as a natural home to wildlife and provide a source of livelihood to the local communities.
The Railway Heritage Park is a railway museum at New Tinsukia Junction railway station, in Assam, India, opened in 2010. The museum is bordered by National Highway 37.

Baghjan Gaon is a village under the Doom Dooma subdivision of the Tinsukia district in Assam, India. As per the 2011 Census of India, Baghjan Gaon has a total population of 4,488 people including 2,244 males and 2,244 females.

Makum (IPA: mɑːkəm) is a town and a town area committee in Tinsukia district in the Indian state of Assam. It should not be mistaken for Makum Pathar (the place near Digboi where, crude oil was first struck in Asia).

The 2020 Assam gas and oil leak, also referred as the Baghjan gas leak, was a blowout and methane leak that happened in Oil India Limited's Baghjan Oilfield in Tinsukia district, Assam, India on 27 May 2020. The blowout occurred at Well No.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dibrugarh Airport (35 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (243 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bherjan-Borajan-Padumoni Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tinsukia, Lido Town, Lido Tikok, Maguri Motapung Beel, Railway Heritage Park, Tinsukia.
